flags.description: Master toggle for tenant-plane writes. Off = every POST/PATCH/DELETE on /api/v1/sports (and disciplines, positions) returns 503. Emergency-only. Reads always continue.

Independent of the `sports_registry_custom` entitlement — this flag globally kills tenant custom creates even for tenants that hold the entitlement. Off = POST refuses with 503. Used during migrations that intentionally freeze the catalog + reshape the underlying schema.
Off = ReconcileSportReferencesJob no-ops. Used during scheduled Wave 3 migrations that intentionally leave downstream rows in temporary drift while a bulk sport_key rename runs.
Off = PurgeArchivedRegistryRowsJob no-ops. Deactivated customs sit past their 730-day retention window. Rare — used during legal holds when custom-sport evidence must be preserved.

Off = every /api/v1/sports/{sport}/deactivate + reactivate route refuses with 503. Applied uniformly across sports, disciplines, positions. Used during safeguarding-audit windows when deactivations must not shift while investigators reconstruct the catalog state.

Off = POST /api/v1/platform/sports/reseed returns 503 + the on-boot listener no-ops. Used to freeze the platform catalog during a coordinated fleet-wide rollout (e.g. adding 10 new sports across all tenants simultaneously requires a coordinated cache invalidation).

Off = GET /api/v1/sports/resolve returns 503. Downstream Wave 3 write paths that call the resolver in-process still work (the resolver's write-path calls bypass this flag) but the HTTP surface is closed. Used when a resolver bug produces wrong answers + the read path is frozen while a fix is deployed.

Off (default) = SeedPlatformSportsCatalogJob runs SYNCHRONOUSLY at boot. On = dispatched to a queue. Default off because catalog absence would break every Wave 3 write; sync boot guarantees the catalog is present before any request lands. On is safe only in dev/staging where a brief catalog-less window is acceptable.
